headline: White House moves toward AI model vetting before public release
body: The White House is considering a pre-release vetting process for AI models, according to reporting from multiple sources covering the policy development. This regulatory approach directly impacts healthcare AI vendors and health systems deploying clinical decision support, diagnostic tools, and administrative automation. Pre-release vetting could extend timelines for healthcare AI products entering the market and create compliance obligations around clinical validation, bias testing, and safety assurance. Healthcare organizations and consulting firms should monitor vetting criteria development, as requirements may reshape vendor qualification processes and purchasing decisions in value-based care environments. The policy signals increased government oversight of AI deployment in regulated sectors, including healthcare.
